To make effective use of public resources, public managers are inventing ways of managing that alter relationships within organizations, between organizations, between sectors, and with the public. Requires rethinking fundamentals such as leadership and motivation.

title: Environmental Theory and Topics
description: Provides an overview of various theoretical approaches to the study of natural and built environments. Cuts across disciplinary boundaries. Canonical studies of the environment and sustainability are examined. Ph.D. level course.

title: Urban Theory and Topics
description: An advanced course for Ph.D. students looking to study canonical and contemporary urban theory that is both interdisciplinary and global.

title: Professional Report
description: Workshop designed to assist M.U.R.P. students in conducting their professional reports.  Students select topics, design projects, conduct professional investigations, and write up reports.

title: Planning Career Development
description: Introduces students to the career options for urban planners. Provides opportunities to meet with professional planners practicing in a variety of settings (public, private, non-profit) and introduces students to professional norms, roles, and career paths.

title: Urban Planning Practicum I
description: Engages students in practical planning projects in the community under a central theme within Planning. Students take a problem-solving approach and employ and further develop data gathering, analysis, graphic and oral communication, public engagement, and report writing skills.

title: Urban Planning Practicum II
description: Engages students in practical planning projects in the community under a central theme within Planning. Students take a problem-solving approach and employ and further develop data gathering, analysis, graphic and oral communication, public engagement, and report writing skills.

title: Doctoral Dissertation Research and Writing
description: Dissertation research with Urban Planning and Public Policy faculty.

title: Research Design
description: Provides training in research design and methods. Students learn how to evaluate the strength of research findings based on the methods used by a researcher and learn to use lessons from the course to develop a research proposal.

title: Directed Studies in Urban Planning
description: Directed studies in Urban Planning.

title: Independent Study in Urban Planning
description: Independent Study in Urban Planning.

title: Fundamentals of Vietnamese
description: Designed for students with little or no exposure to Vietnamese. Natural approach with emphasis on four fundamental skills of listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Conducted in Vietnamese.

title: Fundamentals of Vietnamese
description: Designed for students with little or no exposure to Vietnamese. Natural approach with emphasis on four fundamental skills of listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Conducted in Vietnamese.

title: Fundamentals of Vietnamese
description: Designed for students with little or no exposure to Vietnamese. Natural approach with emphasis on four fundamental skills of listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Conducted in Vietnamese.

title: Intermediate Vietnamese
description: Designed for students to advance their Vietnamese language skills from introductory to intermediate level. Designed to develop writing and reading skills and communicative skills in authentic situations. Students are introduced to aspects of Vietnamese culture relating to lesson topics.

title: Intermediate Vietnamese
description: Designed for students to advance their Vietnamese language skills from introductory to intermediate level. Designed to develop writing and reading skills and communicative skills in authentic situations. Students are introduced to aspects of Vietnamese culture relating to lesson topics.

title: Intermediate Vietnamese
description: Designed for students to advance their Vietnamese language skills from introductory to intermediate level. Designed to develop writing and reading skills and communicative skills in authentic situations. Students are introduced to aspects of Vietnamese culture relating to lesson topics.

title: Vietnamese Peer-to-Peer Language Mentor Program
description: Peer-to-Peer Language Mentor Program in which student mentors work with student mentees to increase the language skills of both participants. One hour weekly meetings. VIETMSE 10A is for language mentors.

title: Vietnamese Peer-to-Peer Language Mentor Program
description: Peer-to-Peer Language Mentor Program in which student mentors work with student mentees to increase the language skills of both participants. One hour weekly meetings. VIETMSE 10B is for language mentees.

title: Vietnamese Culture
description: Vietnamese culture and society in modern and historical contexts through the study of literary works, film, and other media. Philosophy and religious practices may also be examined. Course is taught in English.

title: Art History:Theories and Methods
description: Examines canonical texts and explores current directions in Art History.

title: Theoretical and Historiographical Research Methods
description: Provides students training in constructing and developing a research project integrating theoretical and historiographical methodologies.

title: Visual Studies: Theories and Methods
description: Examines canonical texts and explores current directions in Visual Studies.

title: Graduate Seminar in Visual Studies
description: Studies in selected areas of Visual Studies. Topics addressed vary each quarter.

title: Directed Reading
description: Directed reading on a specific topic agreed upon by student and instructor.

title: Writing Practicum
description: Offered winter quarter each year and taught in a workshop format. Assists students with the preparation and revision of the dissertation prospectus so that they may advance to candidacy.

title: Reading for the Preliminary Examination
description: Directed reading in preparation for the preliminary examination. Formerly Visual Studies 298.

title: Prospectus Research
description: Research and writing of the dissertation prospectus.

title: Dissertation Research
description: Research and writing of the dissertation.

title: The Art of Writing: Poetry
description: Beginners' workshop in the writing of poetry, evaluation of student manuscripts, and parallel readings.

title: The Art of Writing: Prose Fiction
description: Beginners' workshop in fiction writing, evaluation of student manuscripts, and parallel readings.

title: Introduction to Writing and Rhetoric
description: Introduction to the principles of rhetoric through readings and writing assignments across a range of genres, including fiction, non-fiction, journalism and academic essays. Rigorous instruction in composition and style, including organization, paragraph development, sentence-level mechanics.

title: Introduction to Writing and Rhetoric with Lab
description: Introduces the principles of rhetoric through readings and writing assignments across a range of genres. Rigorous instruction in composition and style. Designed for students seeking additional English language support.

title: Intensive Writing
description: Critical reading and analysis conducted through literary, academic, journalistic, and fictional genres with additional instruction in paragraph development and sentence-level mechanics. Students analyze and apply rhetorical principles in a range of writing assignments.

title: Critical Reading and Rhetoric
description: Critical reading and analysis conducted through literary, academic, journalistic, and fictional genres. Students analyze and apply rhetorical principles in a range of writing assignments. Letter Grade with P/NP Option.

title: Argument and Research
description: Intensive instruction in research methods, argumentation, and rhetorical invention. Readings selected from current nonfiction and materials located by students from the University library’s holdings, to include databases across a range of disciplines.

title: Intermediate Poetry Writing
description: Intermediate workshop in the writing of poetry, evaluation of student manuscripts, and parallel readings.

title: Intermediate Fiction Writing
description: Intermediate workshop in the writing of fiction, evaluation of student manuscripts, and parallel readings.

title: Undergraduate Seminar: Applications in Literary Theory and Criticism for Creative Writing
description: Readings in literature, with discussions and written rhetorical responses on their making.

title: Short Story Writing
description: Three-hour workshop in short fiction; discussion of student writing and of relevant literary texts.

title: Novel Writing
description: Three-hour advanced workshop in fiction writing; discussion of student writing and of relevant literary texts.

title: Advanced Expository Writing
description: Study of rhetoric, disciplinary genres, and modes of knowledge production; practice in writing effective prose. Essays of varying lengths, totaling at least 4,000 words.

title: Writing Internship
description: Internships focused on writing. In consultation with a faculty advisor, students create a course from response essays, research essays, and assessment project data. Internships may include editing and publication projects, supervised teaching and tutoring assignments, community literacy projects.

title: Graduate Writers' Workshop (Fiction)
description: Graduate fiction workshop open to students enrolled in the MFA program in Writing.

title: Graduate Writers' Workshop (Fiction)
description: Graduate fiction workshop open to students enrolled in the MFA program in Writing.

title: Graduate Writers' Workshop (Poetry)
description: Graduate poetry workshop open to students enrolled in the M.F.A. Programs in Writing.

title: Writing in Conference (Fiction)
description: Graduate fiction workshop open to students enrolled in the MFA program in Writing.

title: Writing in Conference (Fiction)
description: Graduate fiction workshop open to students enrolled in the MFA program in Writing.

title: Writing in Conference (Poetry)
description: Graduate poetry workshop open to students enrolled in the M.F.A. Programs in Writing.
